items.headline: Major commercial project approved on West San Carlos Street
items.summary: A $696,570 commercial/industrial development is underway at 1300 W San Carlos St, representing significant investment in San José's commercial corridor. This is the only non-residential project in the recent permit batch.

items.headline: Cupertino launches business license amnesty program for shopping centers
items.summary: The city is offering a business license amnesty program targeting shopping center businesses, likely to help previously unlicensed or non-compliant businesses get legitimate. This could affect local retail compliance and city revenue.

items.headline: City announces summer shop local campaign and retail incentives
items.summary: Cupertino is launching a "Summer of Celebrations" campaign to support local retail and small businesses, with new economic development incentives for startups and shops. This signals a coordinated push to strengthen the local business community.

items.headline: Great American Litter Pick-up becomes official annual city event
items.summary: City Council approved the Great American Litter Pick-up as an officially sponsored annual event with city funding and the ability to accept donations. Residents can now participate in an organized, city-backed cleanup program.

items.headline: Community Development Grants Available for Housing Projects
items.summary: The Housing and Human Services Commission will review applications for HOME and Community Development Block Grant (CDBG) funding on March 25. These federal grants support affordable housing and community development projects in Sunnyvale.

items.headline: San Antonio Road area plan study examines land use and transportation
items.summary: The Planning Commission is studying potential land use and transportation changes for the San Antonio Road area. This study could affect how the corridor develops and how residents move through that part of Palo Alto.

items.headline: New retail vitality ordinance proposed to boost local shopping districts
items.summary: The Commission is considering changes to zoning and building regulations designed to implement retail vitality policies from the city's Comprehensive Plan. The ordinance aims to support and revitalize commercial areas across Palo Alto.

items.headline: Campbell updates beekeeping rules with new ordinance
items.summary: The City Council adopted an updated beekeeping ordinance, allowing residents to keep bees with new regulations. This affects homeowners interested in urban beekeeping and pollinator-friendly gardens.
